def: s.f. Pleasantness, agreeableness, delightfulness, winsomeness, beauty, charm.
s.m. Pleasure; sport; dalliance.
adj. & s.m. Pleasing; sporting; — walking or roaming for pleasure, stroll; amusement, sport.
s.m. (f. -trī), One who sports or plays; one who wanders, &c., a wanderer, rover; traveller, sojourner, mere passenger.
s.m. A lover, love; paradise, heaven; a crow; time.

ety: rām, q.v.+āwat = Prk. वत्ता = S. वार्त्ता
def: s.f. The system or tenets of that sect of Hindū ascetics who are followers of Rāmānuj (q.v.s.v. rāma).
more: s. System of a sect of Hindū faḳīrs.

def: s. m. The incarnation of Vish̤ṇu in the person of Rāma. See رام چندر Rāma-chandra.
For this and other words which begin with rām that are not given below, see s.v. rāma.
more: n. f. An epic Sanskrit poem by Vālmīki ; also, the Hindi poem by Tulsī Dās , containing the history of Rām . Sārī Rāmāyan paṛh gaë, par na jānā Sītā kiskī joë! Prov. Read the Ramāyan, and not know whose wife Sita was!
s. f. An epick poem by Vālmīki, containing the history of Rāma. (Poems on the same subject and under the same title have been composed by other authors).

def: n. m. رمض scorched by the sun. The Pagan name was changed in the month of greatest heat. The ninth Mah month, during which Mahomedans are interdicted from eating, drinking, and conjugal duty between dawn and sunset. On the 27th of this month the Qurān began to descend, and every prayer offered up on that night will be answered. Prayers offered up on the 19th and 23rd of the month are believed to be peculiarly efficacious. 1. Ramzān ke namāzī, Moharram ke sipāhī! Prov. In Ramzān a worshipper, in Moharram a soldier! (For the rest of the year he is neither). 2. Yahāṅ to bārah mahīne Ramzān rahtā hai . All the year round it is one perpetual fast (famine) here.

ety: v.n. fr. رمض 'to become intensely heated or hot'
def: s.m. The ninth of the Arabian months (so called because, when they changed the names of the months from the ancient language, they named them according to the seasons in which they fell, and this month agreed with the days of vehement heat. On the 27th of this month the Qorʼān began to descend; and every prayer offered up on that night will, it is believed, be complied with; also, prayers offered up on the 19th, 21st, and 23rd days of this month are supposed to be peculiarly efficacious); — the fast observed during this month, the Moḥammadan Lent (during which Moḥammadans are interdicted from eating, drinking, and conjugal duty, between the morning dawn and appearance of the stars at night).
more: (from رمض To be scorched by the sun; because when the ancient name given by the Pagans was changed, this month happened in the season of greatest heat. Gol. ) s. m. The name of the ninth Muhammadan month, during which they who profess that religion are interdicted from eating, drinking, and conjugal duty, between the morning dawn and appearance of the stars at night: in short, this is their Lent. On the 27th of this month the ḳurʼān began to descend; and, every prayer offered up on that night will be complied with. Also, prayers offered up on the 19th, 21st, and 23d days of this month are supposed to be peculiarly efficacious.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رَمَضَان (ramazān), from Arabic رَمَضَان (ramaḍān).

def: adj. Of or relating to the month, or to the fast, of Ramazān; born in the month of Ramazān; (hence) a proper name.
s.m. A kind of hoe ( = rambhā, q.v.). s.m. An instrument for grubbing or rooting up grass; a spade; a hoe.
Mah. n. f. A fair division of the crop between the landlord and tenant.
s.m. An instrument for grubbing or rooting up grass; a spade; a hoe.
s.f. The plantain, Musa sapientum or M. paradisiaca; — name of a celebrated A psaras, a beautiful nymph of Indra's paradise; a courtesan, a harlot; — delight; — a name of Gaurī.
s.f. A sounding, roaring; lowing (of cattle).
s.f. = رمبها rambhā, q.v. s.m. An instrument for grubbing or rooting up grass; a spade; a hoe.
s.m. = رمبها rambhā, q.v. s.m. An instrument for grubbing or rooting up grass; a spade; a hoe.
n. m. Hymns addressed to Rām .
v.n. To sound, to roar, to bellow, to low (as cattle).
more: v. n. S. रम्भ To bellow; low (a cow). Chor ke peṭ meṅ gāë āp hī āp rambhāë . Prov. A cow lows spontaneously in the thief's stomach. (He betrays himself).

ety: rāṅbh(nā)+Prk. अत्ती = S. आप्तिः (āpi, caus. augment+aff. तिः)
def: s.f. Calling or crying out for redress, a complaint.
v.n. = رانبهنا rāṅbhnā, q.v. v.n. To bellow, roar, cry out; to low (as cattle).
s.m. Slave, servant; a proper name (commonly given to slaves or servants).
more: n. m. S. रमचेट See ṭahlvā. Tohar ram-chervā dīyā kahlā peṭhaulio hal! aibe ne kailā to ham kā karīṅ ? Mag. I told your servant to go to you, if he wont what can I do?
(from राम see S. رام rām, and चेट A slave; q. d. Slave of Rām God)or of A common appellation of a slave.

def: See رمچيرا ramcherā.
Tom, Dick, and Harry; Tag, rag, and bobtail.
n. m. An abbey; a monastery (Mah. khānqāh ).
intj. By Rām ! By God!
s.m. Tag, Rag, and Bobtail; Tom, Dick, and Harry; the populace, the vulgar.
more: Populace, the vulgar. (Compound. of two names peculiar to the lowest ranks; as, in English, Tom, Dick, and Harry; or Tag, Rag, and Bobtail).

ety: for A. رام rāmiṉ, act. part. of رمي 'to throw, cast, &c.'
def: s.m. Thrower, hurler (of javelins, or stones, &c.); shooter; archer; — one who reproaches, or taunts, or upbraids; jester, scoffer; — the constellation Sagittarius.

ety: perf. part. of ramīdan, rt. Zend ram = S. रम्
def: part. Terrified, alarmed, scared, horror-struck, disturbed, afflicted.
more: part. pass. (from رميدن) Terrified, disturbed, afflicted.

ety: v.n. fr. رمّ 'to become old and decayed' (a bone)
def: adj. Old and decayed (bone), carious.

ety: Zend rāma+P. aff. -ish; rt. Zend ram = S. रम्
def: s.f. Repose, rest, ease; joy, mirth, hilarity; harmony, modulation
more: s. 1. Rest, ease. 2. Harmony, modulation. 3. Mirth, hilarity. رامش گر rāmish-gar, A musician, a singer.

def: s.f. lit. 'A pleasing or charming woman'; a Hindū dancing girl; a harlot, prostitute.
more: n. f. A fille de joie ; a Hin. dancing-girl; a prostitute. Tū ne kī rāmjanī, maiṅ ne kīyā rāmjanā . Prov. You've taken a mistress to yourself, I have got a lover too. rām-charchā , n. f. Preaching; a conversation on religious topics.

ety: prob. corr. of ranjhol = ranjol, fr. ranj(nā) = ranj(an) +ol and olā = Prk. उल्ली and उल्लओ = S. उली and उल+कः
def: A kind of ornament for the foot, an anklet.

ety: prob. corr. of ranjhol = ranjol, fr. ranj(nā) = ranj(an) +ol and olā = Prk. उल्ली and उल्लओ = S. उली and उल+कः
def: A kind of ornament for the foot, an anklet.
s. f. Name of a rāgiṇī or musical mode.
s.m. Sand; a tract or collection of sand
more: n. m. Foretelling by figures; geomancy; divination.
s. m. Sand. ¶ ramal, s. m. Geomancy, foretelling by figures, divination.
subs: divining by lines or figures in sand or on the ground, divination (see rammāl).

def: n. f. A national heroic play. It represents the exile of Rāmchandra with his wife and brother, and the war with Rāvan, king of Lankā, in which Rāvan was defeated and killed. It is performed in the first ten days of the light fortnight of the month Āsin .

ety: v.n. fr. رمل 'to weave a mat of palm-leaves,' &c.
def: s.m. A practiser of geomancy, a soothsaver a prophet, a fortune-teller, a conjurer.
more: n. m. رمل he wove. A soothsayer; a fortune-teller.
(adj. sup. from رمل) s. m. A soothsayer, a fortuneteller, prophet, conjurer.

ety: intens. n. fr. رمز 'to make a sign,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Speaking mysteriously, or by signs; — one who speaks mysteriously, &c.; a proposer of enigmas, or riddles, &c., an enigmatist.
more: (adj. superl. from رمز) s. m. 1. A proposer of riddles, enigmas, &c., an enigmatist. 2. Speaking mysteriously.

ety: ? — prob. rāmmūṅ, corr. of rāmmūṅh, 'having a simple or innocent face'; cf. rām-kī jī
def: s.m. (dialec.), A kind of goat.
more: s. m. A kind of goat.

ety: ram˚ = Prk. रम(इ) = S. रम(ते), rt. रम्
def: v.n. To walk about for pleasure, to roam, range, wander; to turn; to go; — v.t. To enjoy, delight in, luxuriate in; to have carnal intercourse with
more: v. n. S. रम rove, revel., Pr. rammaī ; Pālī ramaṇo . 1. To roam; range; wander; rove. 2. To take a fancy to; to be charmed. 3. To enjoy; luxuriate; revel; riot. Sūhā semal dekh-ke, sāiyāṅ, sab hī gaṅvāī buddh, Phūl dekh-ke ram rake, phal kī rahī na suddh . Girdhar Kunḍlī. The parrot saw the ceiba flower and lost his senses quite, His eyes he feasted on the flower, the fruit obscured from sight. 4. To have connexion with. 5. To fill a place; dwell in; occupy. Rūm rūm meṅ ram rahā hai . He dwells in every hair of your body. ramte faqīr, atīt . A wandering Hin. pilgrim.
n. m. 1. Pasture land. 2. A park; a preserve for game ( shikārgāh ).
1. v. n. To turn. 2. To roam, to go, to range, to wander. 3. s. m. A park or chase (for game).
1. ( रमण from r. रम Sport, play) v. a. To enjoy, to have connexion with. 2. s. m. A park.

def: s.m. A place to walk or sport in, a park, a chase, preserve (for game); pasture-land, meadow.
s.m. = ramnā, q.v. v.n. To walk about for pleasure, to roam, range, wander; to turn; to go; — v.t. To enjoy, delight in, luxuriate in; to have carnal intercourse with
more: s. m. A park or chase.

def: n. f. The ninth day of the light fortnight in the Chaitra , the birth-day of Rām. Chait pīchhle pākh janam naumī ko līnā . Bhajan.
n. m. Annona reticulta , Wat.
adv. corr. of rafta-rafta, q.v.
s.f. See رانپي rāṅpī.
more: s. f. A shoemakerʼs knife, a scraper. See رانپي.

def: n. m. Salt ( namak ). rām-raulā , n. m. 1. Religious merry-making; loud chorus of hymns. 2. Noise; uproar; din. rām-sar , n. m. A kind of reed. Tulsī! tum to kahat ho, sangat se sab hot, Bīch ūkh meṅ rām-sar, tehī ras kāhe na hot! Tulsī Dās. Tulsī! you say good company doth every good bestow, No sweet juice in the reed which with the sugar cane doth grow! rām kare! Rām grant!
s. m. 1. The name of a wood. 2. The name of a kind of reed.
n. f. Ocymum gratissimum , Wat.
W. A vegetable; abelmoschos esculentus , Wat.; okhra or gobbo, known as ladies' fingers.
s. f. The name of a vegetable, Ochra (Hibiscus esculentus).
adj. = ramaṇīya, q.v. adj. To be enjoyed, enjoyable, pleasant, agreeable, delightful, charming, beautiful, lovely, handsome.
s.m. Name of a Varsha; name of one of the minor divisions (dvīp) of the world (lying to the north of Ilāvrit).

ety: inf. n. of رمز 'to make a sign'
def: s.f. Sign, indication, nod, wink, hint, insinuation, innuendo, ambiguous expression, double entendre, mysterious allusion, riddle, enigma; sarcasm, irony
more: n. f. 1. See اشاره 6, 7, & بهير 3, 4. 2. Inuendo; double entendre. Yeh āpas meṅ ramzoṅ kī bāteṅ huīṅ, Ishāroṅ kī bāham jo ghāteṅ huīṅ . Mīr Hasan. ramz chal'nā, mārnā, yā pheṅknā , v. a. To speak at one; make allusions.
s. f. A wink, nod, sign, hint, riddle, enigma, mysterious allusion, ambiguous expression, sarcasm. (Plur. رموز).
subs: ramz ćalānā, ramz mārnā, To throw out hints or insinuations, to make allusions, to speak in innuendoes, to speak (at one).

def: of or pertaining to a symbol or symbols
wety: Borrowed from Arabic رَمْزِيّ (ramziyy). By surface analysis, رمز (ramz, “symbol”) + ـی (-ī).

ety: Pehl. rān; Zend rāna, rt. ar = S. ar (r̤iṇoti)
def: s.f. The thigh (syn. jāṅgh)
more: n. f. The thigh ( jāṅgh ). 1. Paisā gāṅṭh kā, ghoṛā rān kā . Prov. Money in the pocket, the horse under the rider's thigh. 2. Ān se māre, tān se māre, phir na mare to rān se māre . Prov. With her eyes she 'll slay, with song she 'll slay, if still he die not under her thighs she 'll slay. rān tale ānā , v. n. (The harlot) To be ridden; to be had. rān tale dabānā , v. a. lit . to press one under one's thigh. 1. To subdue; bring down on one's knees. 2. To ride; mount a horse. 3. To get on (a woman).

1. s. f. m. The thigh. ران تلي دبانا rān tale dabānā, (i. q. عاجزکرنا) To subdue, humiliate, make weak (?). ران سي ران باندهنا rān se rān bāndhnā, v. a. To guard or watch diligently. 2. part. in compos. (from راندن) Driving.
subs: v.t. 'To press under the thigh,' to have completely in (one's) power, to subdue; to mount, ride (a horse)
(-kī), To keep close to, to keep close watch over (one), to guard or watch diligently.

ety: fr. rān-dan, contrac. of rav-ān-dan, caus. of raftan; rt. Zend harp or hrap? = S. सर्प्
def: part. Driving, driving away; managing, conducting (used in comp., e.g. kām-rān, 'pushing on desire,' prosperous, successful)
subs: v.t. (local), To drive out, to expel.

ety: Prk. रणं or अरणं; S. अरण्यं
def: s.m. Wood, forest; desert, waste, wilderness
more: n. m. S. रण 1. A wood; forest; waste. 2. War ( jang ). 3. A battle field. 1. Nāth! kaṭak meṅ so kapi nāhīṅ Jo na tumahiṅ jītahiṅ ran māṅhīṅ! Rāmāyan. O sire! in all Ram's force of monkeys there's not one But might your armies rout and shake your power and throne. 2. Sūrā ran meṅ jāë-ke kāīṅ ḍhūṅḍe sāth? Sāthī tere tīn haiṅ, hiyā, kaṭārī, hāth . In battle field the brave no help demand, Three aids are theirs, stout heart, and sword, and hand. 4. A mark ( dāg ). Mātā kā ran. ran-ban , n. m. A wilderness. ran-bhūm ; P. maidān-i-jaṅg , n. f. A battle field. ran paṛnā , v. n. 1. To be fought (a battle). 2. To be slain (numbers). ran-jīt , n. m. A victor; conqueror. Tauṅh baṛā ran-jīt saë! ( Ironic .) Rus. ran-dhīrā , n. m. A gallant, brave man.
s. m. Battle, war, combat. رنبهوم रणभुमि raṇ-bhūmi, Field of battle. رن کهندل raṇ-khandal, s. (dakh. i. q. مقتل) Place of slaughter (?).

def: v. a. (dakh.) To expel.
adj. Wild, growing spontaneously (syn. ḵẖud-rau).

ety: fem. of ارعن arʻan, v.n. fr. رعن 'to be soft,' &c.: — applied in Persian to masc. and fem.
def: adj. Moving gracefully; graceful, lovely, beautiful, adorned; delicate, tender.
more: (from رعن To be relaxed or languid. Heb. To be green, to flourish) adj. 1. Moving gracefully. 2. Beautiful, lovely, graceful, delicate.

def: s.m. = rāṇā, q.v.; — rāṇ, s.f. = rāṇḍ, q.v.
s.m. Sound, noise; battle, war, combat, fight, conflict; battle-field ( = raṇ-bhūmi);-a mosquito; anxiety:-raṇāngan (˚na+an˚), s.m. Battle-place, field of battle
subs: vulg. ran-bhūm, s.f. The region or locality of war, battle-field
Fight to take place, battle to be fought; — to lie in the battle-field, to be slain in battle
raṇ-dhīrā, adj. & s.m. Firm, steady, or composed in battle; — a calm and cool soldier, a gallant or brave man
s.m. 'Battle-pillar,' monument of war or battle, trophy, column
raṇ-siṅghā, raṇ-siṅhā, s.f. Battle-conch, war-trumpet, trumpet
adj. & s.m. Desirous of war, wishing to fight; — a warlike man
s.m. 'War-bracelet,' a kind of bracelet used as an offensive weapon in battle

def: s.m. Title of a prince or rājā (among Hindūs, esp. Rājpūts); name of a tribe of Rājpūts.
more: n. m. S. राजन् A Rājput title of a prince.
s. m. A Hindū title of a prince or rājā.

ety: raʻnā+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Gracefulness of motion, graceful gait; grace, loveliness, beauty.
s.m. See s.v. raṇ. s.f. The thigh (syn. jāṅgh)
subs: v.t. 'To press under the thigh,' to have completely in (one's) power, to subdue; to mount, ride (a horse)
(-kī), To keep close to, to keep close watch over (one), to guard or watch diligently.

def: (comp. of अरण्य Forest, and वन Forest) s. m. A wilderness.
s.m. corr. of ranwās, q.v. s.m. The seraglio of a Rājā; a harem; a gynaeceum.

ety: rāṅbh˚ = Prk. रंभ(इ) = S. रम्भ(ते), rt. रम्भ्
def: v.n. To bellow, roar, cry out; to low (as cattle).
more: v. n. S. रम्भण To low as a cow. Pau phāṭī, pīlī phaṭī, galyan rāṅbhī gāëṅ, Uṭho su-buddhī, budh do! kā bidh ghar ne jāëṅ ? The grey dawn's pierced the sky, the golden toe, the kine low in the lane, Get up, wise one! your wisdom use! how now unseen my home regain?
v. n. To low, to bellow, to cry out.

def: s.m. A particle, a little, the least bit, somewhat.
corr. of, 1˚ ranjit; 2˚ raćit, qq.v.
v.n. (rustic), 1˚ = rāćnā; — 2˚ = raćnā, qq.v. v.n. To be affected or imbued (with love, or passion), to be strongly attached (to).
s.f. = rāṅdh, q.v. s.f. Quarrel, dispute, contention, strife (syn. rāṛ)
more: n. f. See jhagṛā. rāṅd kāṭnā , v. a. To settle or dispose of a quarrel. De dilā-ke rāṅd kāṭo!

( In comp .) ranḍ , n. f. S. रण्डा 1. A widow ( bidhvā ). 1. Ham kyā rāṅḍ ke jaṅvāi haiṅ ? Prov. Do you take me for a widow's spawn? 2. Wohī rāṅḍ kī rāṅḍ, wohi bābal-pīṭī . Prov. Call her the widow's daughter, call her an orphan fatherless, it is the same. 3. kālā hiran mat māryo, sattar hongī rāṅḍ . Bhartarī. Slay not the black buck! seventy does will widowed be. 4. Rāṅḍeṅ to bahuterī raheṅ jo ranḍve rahne deṅ . Prov. Widows many might still be if widowers would but let them be. 5. Mujh rāṅḍ kā na koī dhīr kā dharaiyā, na bāt kā puchhaiyā! Wom. A widow forlorn! No one to comfort me or care about me! 6. Rāṅḍ se baṛh-ke kosnā nahīṅ . Wom. Prov. No curse so great as wishing one a widow. 2. A term of abuse in addressing women. 3. A woman; wench. Sir mūṅḍūṅ us rāṅḍ kā jo khasam se pahle khāë! Shaven be the head of her who eats before her husband hath first dined! rāṅḍā napūtī , Wom. n. f. li…
subs: To put an end to strife, to settle a dispute.

def: s.m. = randā, 'hole,' q.v. part. adj. Driven, driven out, expelled; rejected, cast off, forsaken; — s.m. One who is expelled, or rejected, &c.
more: n. m. The trunk; a headless body.
(In comp.) n. f. S. रण्डा A widow. See راندَ Kuttī ranḍ! Oh widow bitch!

def: s.m. The castor-oil plant, Palma christi, or Ricinus communis.
more: s. m. The name of a tree. (Palma christi. Ricinus vulgaris).

def: adj. & s.m. Faithless; barren; — one who neglects his religious duties; one who dies without male issue.
s.f. A widow; woman; a term of abuse in addressing women, a slut
more: s. f. 1. A widow. رانڐ کا سانڐ rānḍ kā sānḍ, Son of a widow, a spoilt child. 2. A woman.
subs: s.f. The state of being a widow and childless; wishing a woman to be reduced to that state (as women do when quarrelling)
s.m. lit. 'A widow's stallion,' a gallant, a cavaliere serviente; a base, contemptible fellow; — a spoilt child

def: s.f. A widow (used in comp.)
subs: raṇḍ-sāla, s.m. Widow's dress, widow's weeds.

def: s.m. Opening, fissure, hole; loop-hole, embrasure, air-hole (see randhra).
more: (from رنديدن To polish) s. m. A plane (for smoothing wood).
s. (dakh.) A profligate, debauchee ( Harris ): see رند rind.

def: s.m. = P. رنده randa, q.v.
s.m. = randā, and randhra, qq.v. part. adj. Driven, driven out, expelled; rejected, cast off, forsaken; — s.m. One who is expelled, or rejected, &c.
more: n. m. رنديدن to polish. A carpenter's plane; a planing tool; shaping machine. randa phernā, yā k. , v. a. To plane.
(from رنديدن To polish) s. m. A plane (for smoothing wood).
s. m. 1. A hole. 2. A loophole, an embrasure, an airhole.

def: s.f. A widow; a term of abuse; — the plant Salvinia cucullata; — name of a species of Sanskrit metre.
adv. 1. Barren, unproductive (as the male palm, etc.) 2. ( raṅḍuā ) A widower.
adj. Barren, unproductive (as a tree, e.g. the male palm, &c.; also applied to the mango-fish without roes); — s.m. A widower ( = ranḍuʼā).
more: adj. Barren. (The mangoe fish without roes are so called: and, the word is particularly applied to unproductive trees, such as the male palm, &c.).

ety: perf. part. of rāndan; see rāṅdnā, and rān
def: part. adj. Driven, driven out, expelled; rejected, cast off, forsaken; — s.m. One who is expelled, or rejected, &c.
more: adj. Driven out; excommunicated. rāṅda-i-dargāh , adj. Accursed by Heaven.
part. pass. (of راندن) Driven, driven out, rejected, expelled.

ety: rt. = S. रद्; whence P. randīdan
def: s.m. A joiner's or carpenter's plane; a rasper, grater
subs: (-par), or randa karnā (-ko), To pass the plane (over), to plane.

def: s.m. A barren tree.
n. m. A barren tree.
s.m. Widowhood ( = ranḍāpā, q.v.).
n. m. Widowhood. Aisne put se raṅḍāpā kaṭāī ? Mag. Will such a son her widowhood console?
n. m. Widowhood.

ety: raṇḍā+pā, or pan, or panā = Prk. प्पअं, or प्पणं or प्पणअं = S. त्व+कं, or त्वं (Vedic त्वनं); — ˚pro = paro, prob. = Prk. प्पडो = S. त्व+र+कः
def: s.m. Widowhood.

For the barber I'll prepare dal-rice, for my brother of my mother's womb a rice-milk mess, for Sāvan rains have come. 2. Kanth merā postī, solah chane kī khāë, Ek dinā rāṅdhī khīchṛī, kūe ḍūban jāë . Wom. song. My husband opium eats, pulse cakes sixteen but him content, One day dal-rice I cooked, to drown himself he straightway went.

The buffalo is dry, from whence the child's milk-mess provide? A drop of milk no one will give, the neighbours all I've tried. 2. Raṅdhāo na sijāo, phuaṛ ne khuāo! Rus. Wom. Phāgan meṅ chain kare raṅḍvā, jo nārī nikse dhore kar us se bagal bhare raṅḍvā . Rus. song. In March gay widowers disporting see, No female comes in their vicinity But what these widowers with them make free. 3. Raṅḍve kī khāṭ paras ghālo, ghar sotā yoh sohātā nāhīṅ, or kitaë is ne ṭālo! Rus. Wom. song. The widower's bed just in the open lay, Within the house to sleep's not meet, do, pray! Bestow him somewhere else just right away! 2. (Sant. ḍaṅgvā ,) A batchelor ( kuārā ). Yeh to sadā kā raṅḍrā bhaṅḍvā hai . Just like a widower, through life a rover.

def: s.m. more: n. m. S. रञ्ज to dye, Pālī, rāgo . 1. Color; dye; paint; hue; tint; complexion. 2. A suit at cards. Raṅg kavvā sā, aur Mahtāb nām . Prov. As black as a crow, and moon-faced called. 3. Aspect; countenance; look; view. Aë Hind! terā woh raṅg kahāṅ hai? Pahlā sā terā woh ḍhaṅg kahāṅ hai ? Sohan Lāl. Oh India! where is now the beauty thou once wore! Where now the grace and mien thou didst display afore! 4. Manner; mode ( ḍhab , 2.). 5. Condition; state. 6. Bloom; beauty. Āj kal us par raṅg hai . 7. Sport; entertainment; dance and song. 8. Pleasure; enjoyment; bliss. 1. Mūṅh surkhī se gulnār huë, tan kesar kī sī kyārī hai, Ye rūp jhamaktā dikhlāyā, ye raṅg dikhāyā Holī ne! Nazīr, Each maid a bright pomegranate flower, parterres of crimson seen! Such beauty lighted up the scene, such Holi's! splendent sheen! 2. Āshiq ko dikhātī hai ajab raṅg javānī! Wondrous the bloom that beauty shows to love…

wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀭𑀁𑀕 (raṃga), from Sanskrit रङ्ग (raṅga), which is derived from the root रञ्ज् (rañj). Also influenced by Classical Persian رنگ (rang). In either case, ultimately from Proto-Indo-Iranian *(H)rag-, *(H)ranǰ- (“to dye, color”), of unclear origin. Cognate with Gujarati રંગ (raṅg), Marathi रंग (raṅga), Punjabi رَن٘گ (raṉg) and Odia ରଙ୍ଗ (raṅga).

def: n. f. The Hin. Holī ceremony of sprinkling the ṭesū color and abīr over one's relatives and friends. raṅg phīkā, pīlā, yā māṅd paṛnā , v. n. Bhaṅg kahe, 'maiṅ raṅgī-jaṅgī; post kahe 'maiṅ Shāh-jahāṅ;' Afīm kahe 'maiṅ chhunnī begam,' mujh ko khā-ke jāë kahāṅ? Bacahanalia. Says Bhang 'I'm Bacchus strong,' says Poppy head 'The world's king I,' Says Opium 'I'm the scarlet dame, who tastes me ne'er can fly. raṅg chaṛhā'nā , v. a. To dye; paint (wood, tin). raṅḍ chaṛh'nā , v. a. 1. To be painted; dyed. 2. To get a color; to be flushed. 3. To be flushed with liquor; to be merry. raṅg chhiṛak'nā, raṅg-pāshī k. , v. a. To sprinkle color. Har ān khushī se āpas meṅ sab haṅs-haṅs raṅg chhiṛakte hoṅ, Rukhsār gulāloṅ se gul-gūṅ, kapṛoṅ se raṅg ṭapakte hoṅ . Nazir. With laughter glad the water red is squirted o'er and o'er, Red-powdered cheeks pomegranate flowers, their garments dripping o'er. raṅg-dār , adj. Colored; gay; bright; showy; fine. raṅg dekhnā , v. n. 1. To consider the consequences. 2. To remain neutral. raṅg ḍhaṅg . Appearance; aspect; phenomena (حال 1.) raṅg ras , W.; raṅg chaṅg , E., n. f. Pleasure ( raṅg . 7.). Chār din kā raṅg chaṅg! chhoṛ dahijār mor saṅg! Prov. Four days' enjoyment you would have! Begone incestuous beast! raṅg-rasyā , n. m. A wanton; lascivious person. Malyā kā chhokrā baṛā raṅg-rasyā, woh to lemuā khilāë man moh līnā re! Wom. song. The gardener's son's a wanton warm and gay, He gives me limes and takes my heart away. raṅg-ralyāṅ , n. f. Sports; pastimes; music and dancing; feast and fun; merriment; revelry; rejoicings. raṅg rūp . 1. Aspect; appearance; character. Sau makr a fun banānā, sau raṅg rūp karnā, Āshiq ko har tarah se khūbāṅ kī dīd karnā . Nazīr. 2. Bright complexion; the flush of health; bloom. Raṅg rūp dekh-kar na bhūliye! Prov. Let not the world's gay colors bright beguile thy heart! raṅg rūp nikalnā, yā raṅg nikalnā , v. n. To come out well (color or beauty).

'Get up, get up, my sister-in-law! some cloth get me and suits make up! your husband's sister from afar's your guest.' n. m. S. रस, Pālī, rasa . 1. Any liquid or fluid. 2. Juice; humour; sap; serum. 3. Essence ( sat ). Rām punīt bikhaë ras rūkhe, Lolup bhūp bhog ke bhūkhe . Rāmāyan. Rām pure averse from worldly joys, Bhup sunk in sensuous alloys. 4. Semen virile . 5. Flavour; savour; taste. 6. Sentiment; feeling; passion; love. The six physical tastes ( khaṭ-ras ), as sweet, sour, salt, bitter, acrid, astringent; hence a symbol for 6 in Hindī poetry. Nine sentiments are usually enumerated in Poetry, viz. shringār , love or the intercourse of lovers; hāsya , mirth, the ludicrous style; karunā , pity, the pathetic; raudra , anger, describing or exciting rage or indignation; vīra , heroism, the heroic or epic, describing deeds of courage and martial prowess; bhayānak , terror, the terrible; vib- hatsa , disgust or aversion; adbhut , wonder, the marvellous; shānt , tranquillity or contentment. Aṅgyā maske, j…

s. m. 1. Juice, humour, essence. 2. Flavour, taste, of which are reckoned six kinds, viz. sweet, sour, salt, bitter, acrid, astringent. 3. Taste, sentiment or emotion, as an object of poetry or other composition: eight sentiments are usually enumerated, viz. (1.) शृंगार sr̤ingāra, Love; (2.) हास्य hāsya, Mirth; (3.) करुणा karuṇā, Tenderness; (4.) रौद्र raudra, Anger; (5.) वीर vīra, Heroism; (6.) भयानक bhayānaka, Terrour; (7.) वीभत्स vībhatsa, Disgust; (8.) अद्भुत adbhuta, Surprise; शांत sānta, Tranquillity or content, or वात्सल्य vātsalya, Paternal tenderness, is sometimes considered as the ninth. 4. Relish, enjoyment. 5. Quicksilver, mercury. رس گهولنا ras gholnā (dakh. i. q. الفت هونا) Friendship or intimacy to be or become, to become intimate (?)

(from رسيدن) part. act. used in compos. Arriving, happening, touching at, causing to arrive, one to whom (a thing) arrives; as داد رس dād-ras, An administerer of justice. فرياد رس faryād-ras, One whom lamentation reaches, i. e. a protector, a preserver.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀭𑀲 (rasa), from Sanskrit रस (rása).

def: A high-bred horse.

s.f. Heap, mass, pile, accumulation, stock; a heap of unthreshed corn; a heap of husked grain (the produce of the first treading of the ears); a sign of the zodiac, &c. (see rāśi); — interest, usury; — adoption (of a son)
subs: (-ko), To agree (with, — as climate, medicine, &c.), to suit; to be auspicious; to be set to rights, be made well, be cured (rās, in this phrase, is supposed by some to be a contrac. of rāst, q.v.)
rās-ćakkar, s.m. The zodiac; the ecliptic
s.m. The obliquity of the ecliptic
(-kī), To come, or be, under the same constellation or star; to be in harmony or accord (with)

def: s.m. Noise, din; quarrel, fray ( = rāṛ).
more: Wom. n. m. Dispute; quarrel. Mat kāchī toṅh rakhyo ghānī, Nāṅh karūṅgī ant rāsā, Gur rākh, pakāvā, mat kāchā! Rus. V. m. Dont leave the jaggery under-boiled, I say, Or there will be 'twixt us a serious fray, Dont, boiler, under-boil the juice, I say!

def: s.m. Gravy, soup; sauce, &c. ( = ras, q.v.). s.m. The head; head (of cattle, i.e. one ox, one horse); top, summit, apex, vertex; end, extremity; head-land, promontory, cape
more: adj. 1. Penetrating; quick of apprehension. 2. Welcome; well-received; popular.
1. part. pres. (of رسيدن) In compos. Arriving, attaining (or, perhaps, from رسانيدن) causing to arrive. 2. adj. Quick of apprehension, penetrating, skilful, capable, clever.

def: s.f. The earth, ground, soil; the tongue; the lower world, hell; a mythical stream supposed to flow round the earth and the atmosphere; — a vine, a grape (cf. raz); name of several plants; — rasā-tal, rasā-tal-lok, s.m. Name of the lowest of the seven hells or regions under the earth (see pātāl); the lower world or hell in general (it is the abode of various monstrous and demoniacal beings, as Nāg, Asur, Daitya, &c.; but is not to be confounded with Narak or the place of punishment).

s.m. Sap, juice; syrup; a liquid, fluid; water; liquor; gravy, soup, broth; (met.) the best or prime part (of anything), essence, marrow, pith; potion, poisonous drink, poison; constituent fluid or essential juice (of the body); humour; serum; chyle; semen virile; quicksilver, mercury; exudation; gum, myrrh; — taste, savour, flavour, relish (of which there are six kinds, viz. sweet, sour, salt, bitter, acrid, astringent; hence, in Hindī poetry), a symbolical expression for the number six; — taste, inclination, appetite, enjoyment (of anything); love, affection, desire; pleasure, charm, grace, piquancy, elegance, beauty; sweetness, softness, spirit, wit, taste, style, character; — taste, sentiment, feeling, emotion, pathos, affection, passion (ten such sentiments are enumerated in dramatic composition, viz. śr̤ingār-ras, 'the erotic sentiment,' 'love'; hāsya-ras, 'the sentiment of humour,' 'comic vein,' 'facetiousness,' &c.; karuṇa-ras, 'the pathetic sentiment'; vīr-ras, 'heroism'; raudra, 'indignation, anger'; bhayānak, 'terror'; bībhatsā, 'disgust'; adbhut, 'wonder, surprise'; śānt, 'tranquillity or contentment'; vātsalya, 'paternal tenderness')
subs: (˚sa+ad), adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Taking or drinking juice, absorbing moisture; — sucker or drinker of juice, &c.
(˚sa+āl˚), s.m. The seat or abode of the Rasas; the seat of enjoyments
(˚sa+an˚), s.m. A sort of collyrium prepared from the calx of brass with Amomum anthorrhiza (or, according to some, prepared from vitriol of copper with the addition of Curcuma)

vulg. rasāʼin (˚sa+ay˚), s.m. Alchemy; chemistry; a mineral or metallic preparation; the employment of mercury in medicine, or for magical purposes; a medicine supposed to prevent old age and prolong life, an elixir, elixir vitæ; buttermilk; poison; — an alchemist; a name of Garuḍ
(-kā), To turn base metals into gold or silver
s.f. The science of alchemy, or of chemistry
s.m. An alchemist ( = rasāyan), a chemist; — s.f. A canal or channel for the fluids (of the body), a vessel conveying chyle, a lacteal or absorbent vessel
adj. (f. -ī), Full of juice or sap; full of taste or sentiment; juicy, succulent; liquid; luscious; tasteful; sensuous, voluptuous
(-meṅ), To be or become full of juice, or sap, or serum; to become juicy; — to attain to maturity or puberty; to be pleased or delighted (with), to receive enjoyment (from)
s.m. A muriate of mercury (formed by subliming in close vessels a mixture of sulphur, mercury, and common salt)
v.n. Juice to drop or trickle (from); to be ripe (lit. & fig.), to be mature, be in full bloom or vigour; to be full of sexual desire

(vulg. rasaggya), adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Knowing tastes, discriminating or appreciating flavour or excellence, knowing what gives real enjoyment; knowing the true essence (of), capable of discerning the spirit or beauty (of); — a man of discrimination; a writer who understands the different sentiments (ras), a poet; an alchemist, one who understands the magical properties of mercury; a physician, a preparer of mercurial and chemical compounds
vulg. rasa-gyatā, s.f. Knowledge of flavours; acquaintance with the true essence of things; poetical skill or taste; alchemy
s.m. A sort of factitious cinnabar made with zinc, mercury, blue vitriol, and nitre fused together
vulg. ras-kapūr, s.m. Corrosive white sublimate, or a muriate of mercury similar to it (made with sulphur, mercury, and common salt)
s.m. 'A mine of ras,' an epithet of Krishṇa
s.m. Rice-milk, rice boiled in milk, rice and milk
(-kā), To take or draw out the essence (of); to extract pleasure (from), to enjoy
s.f. Cessation or loss of taste, flavour, &c.
adj. & s.m. Quarrel-exciting; — a fomenter of quarrels, mischief-maker
ras-vat (f. ras-vatī), adj. Having juice or sap, juicy, succulent; well-flavoured, tasteful, agreeable, savoury, luscious; — tasty, charming, elegant, piquant; spirited, witty
s.f. An agreeable or charming woman; — a cook-room, kitchen
(˚sa+in˚), s.m. 'Chief of fluids or metals,' quicksilver; the philosopher's stone (the touch of which turns iron, &c. to gold).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀭𑀲 (rasa), from Sanskrit रस (rása).

ety: imperf. part. of ras-īdan; see ras
def: part. & adj. Arriving, attaining; causing to arrive (used as last member of compounds); quick of apprehension, acute, sharp, penetrating, skilful, capable, clever; — mixing or mingling (with); amiable; well-received, welcome.
s.m. (f. -ī), An ass.
s.f. Coming in, income, import; revenue; store of grain (laid up for an army, or camp, &c.), grain, provision, supplies; allowance of food, ration; share, portion, quota, contribution
more: n. f. 1. Imports, esp. of grain. Āj kal rasad band hai . 2. A store of grain, etc; commissariat supplies for an army, etc. 3. Allowance of food; rations. 4. Share; contribution. rasad pahuṅchānā , v. n. To furnish supplies for an army, etc. rasad-rasāṅ , n. m. One who provides supplies. ras'adī , adj. Rateable; proportionate; progressively increasing or decreasing. hissa-i-rasadī , n. m. A proportionate share.
s. f. Store of grain laid in for an army, &c. grain, provision.
subs: s.f. Increase of income or revenue
s.m. A provider of supplies, one who causes supplies to reach (a camp, &c.).

def: adj. Proportionate, rateable; progressively increasing or decreasing.
s. f. 1. Access, entrance, arriving. 2. Wisdom, quickness of apprehension, penetration, cleverness, skill.

ety: abst. s. fr. rasā, q.v.; and see ras
def: s.f. Arriving; entrance, access; accessibleness; reach, compass; — quickness of apprehension, sharpness, acuteness, penetration, cleverness, skill, talent, ability, wisdom; propriety, fitness.
more: n. f. 1. Entrance; audience ( pahuṅch ). 2. Power of mind; quickness of apprehension; penetration. 3. Popularity.

def: s.m. or f. pl. (of risāla or risālat), Messages; letters.

corr. of rasik, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Tasty, savoury, flavoured; tasteful (as a composition, &c.); full of feeling or passion, sentimental, impassioned; spirited, witty; graceful, elegant; taking pleasure (in); fanciful; lustful; voluptuous; — a man full of feeling or passion; a man of pleasure, a sensualist, a libertine; — a horse; an elephant.

ety: Prk. रसालो = S. रसालः; see ras
def: adj. & s.m. Having juice, juicy, succulent; savoury, &c. ( = rasīlā, q.v.); — the sugar-cane; the mango tree.
more: n. m. The sugarcane; the mango tree.

def: s.f. Curds mixed with sugar and spices.
s.m. 1˚ = ras-ālay, q.v.s.v. ras; — 2˚ = corr. of risāla, 'a troop of horse,' &c.
s.f. Any tubular vessel of the body (esp. one conveying the fluids), a vein, artery; a nerve, tendon.

ety: v.n. fr. رسن 'to tie with a rope'
def: s.m. Rope, cord (syn. rassā)
more: s. m. String, cord, rope. (Plur. ارسان arsān ). رسن باز rasan-bāz, A ropedancer. رسن ساز rasan-sāz, Ropemaker.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رَسَن (rasan, “rope; string”). Doublet of رَشْمِی (raśmī) and رَشْنا (raśnā) (borrowings from Sanskrit र॒श्मि (raśmí) and र॒श॒ना (raśanā́) respectively).

def: ( In comp .) Causing to arrive; bearing; as chiṭṭhī-rasāṅ a letter-peon.
s.m. contrac. of rasāʼin, q.v.

ety: fr. rasān-īdan, caus. of ras-īdan; see ras
def: part. & s.m. Causing to arrive, conveying, bearing; — one who conveys, bearer (used as last member of compounds, e.g. ćiṭṭhī-rasān, 'Letter-carrier,' 'letter-peon').
more: n. m. Slowness; quietness; mildness; gentleness. rasān meṅ, rasāṅ rasāṅ, rasān se, rase rase , adv. Slowly; quietly; easily.
part. (act. of رسانيدن or pres. of رسيدن) in compos. Causing to arrive, arriving.

def: s.f. The tongue.
more: n. f. S. रस taste. The tongue.

def: s.f. A woman's zone or girdle.
v.t. To solder.
s.f. A woman's zone or girdle.
s.f. corr. of rasanā, q.v. v.t. To solder.
s.f. Language, speech.
s.m. An alchemist ( = rasāyanī, q.v.s.v. ras). s.m. Noise, din; quarrel, fray ( = rāṛ).

ety: prob. S. रक्ष+āʼo = Prk. एअव्वं = S. इतव्यं
def: s.m. Reclaiming waste land; dressing land (crushing clods and smoothing the soil and letting it lie thus for some days before sowing).
more: n. Dressing a field; pulverising and smoothing the soil, and leaving it exposed to the air some days before sowing.

ety: v.n. fr. رصّ 'to stick or join together'
def: s.m. Lead; pewter; tin.
more: (from رصّ raṣṣa, He joined or united firmly. Heb. To beat, to impinge; which may produce either consolidation or fracture) s. m. 1. Tin. 2. Lead. ¶ raṣṣāṣ, A tinman.

def: s.m. See s.v. rasā. s.m. Noise, din; quarrel, fray ( = rāṛ).
more: n. f. रसा earth, तल bottom; Pālī, rasātalam . The 5th infernal region ( pattāl ).

def: s.m. = rās, q.v. s.m. The head; head (of cattle, i.e. one ox, one horse); top, summit, apex, vertex; end, extremity; head-land, promontory, cape
s.f. Excrescence, tumour, boil, wen; proud flesh.
more: n. f. Excrescence; wen; proud flesh.

ety: ras, q.v.+aut = āwat = Prk. वत्ता = S. वार्त्ता
def: s.f. The juice of the box-thorn, or of the Amomum anthorhizum (which is used medicinally, especially as a collyrium); — (fig.) anything bitter, acrid, or disagreeable.
more: E. n. f. Barberry; Berberis lycium (extract), Wat . a kind of collyrium, extracted from the root of the Amomum anthorhizum . Roxb. rasaut' , adj. Bitter; acrid; harsh. Saut kī bāt rasaut . Prov. The co-wife's words are bitter as gall.
s. f. A kind of collyrium, extracted from the root of the Amomum anthorhizum: Roxb.

ety: prob. a contrac. of ras+ćāwal; or = rasāl (with w inserted
def: s.m. Rice boiled in the juice of sugar-cane.
more: n. m. A dish of rice and the juice of sugar-cane boiled together.
s. m. A kind of dish (rice and the juice of sugar-cane boiled together).

def: s.m. Alchemy; chemistry, &c. (see s.v. ras); — going slowly, slowness; gentleness, mildness; quietness ( = rasān)
more: n. f. S. रस meroury. 1. Alchemy. 2. A mineral or metallic preparation. rasāyan banānā , v. a. To turn base metals into gold or silver. rasāyan mel, kan-lāg , Chemical affinity.
s. m. 1. Chymistry. 2. A medicine composed of mercury or other metals.
subs: rasāʼin-se, adv. Slowly, gently, softly, quietly, easily.

def: see s.v. rasa, ras. s.m. Noise, din; quarrel, fray ( = rāṛ).

ety: ras, contrac. of rassā, q.v.+Prk. वट्टओ = S. वर्तकः
def: s.m. A maker of rope, cord, &c.; rope-maker.
s.f. = raswat or rasaut, q.v.
more: 1. ( रसवती ) See رسوت rasaut. 2. ( रसवर्त्ति ) A port-fire.
